meta_description: Instant quotes for anodized parts. Anodizing type II, type III (Hardcoat), Anodizing type II (Colors: Red, Blue, Orange, Gold, Black, Clear), Anodizing type III (Black, Natural).(Hubs is now Protolabs Network.)

sections.heading: Aluminum anodizing services
sections.text: Surface finishes Aluminum anodizing services We offer anodizing type II and III, which are ideal surface finishes for aluminum parts. Anodizing strengthens aluminum parts and is available in several colors, depending on the type. All types of anodizing will increase the lead time and cost per part. Get instant quote

sections.heading: Design considerations for aluminum anodizing
sections.text: Design considerations for aluminum anodizing Anodizing involves suspending parts via a âjigâ or ârack.â This will leave marks on your part where anodizing wasnât possible. If there are areas on which you canât accept âjigâ marks, please remember to include a technical drawing indicating these areas and follow our cosmetic procedure. Learn more about this on our surface finishing page Anodizing insulates your parts, reducing their conductivity. Masking is not required by default, as type II anodizing doesnât raise the thickness of your part significantly. Remember to indicate in your CAD file whether you want specific part areas to be masked or plugged. Type III anodizing,...

sections.heading: Our anodizing process
sections.text: Our anodizing process Anodizing is an electrolytic process both for protecting aluminum parts against wear and corrosion and for improving cosmetic appearances. Letâs break down the basic steps for anodizing a part: Connect the part to an anode Submerge the part in acidic electrolyte solution Apply a cathode to a metal electrode in the solution Positive ions react with negative O2 ions and the surface becomes porous This grows a layer of aluminum oxide on the part Use corrosion inhibitors or colored dyes for cosmetic purposes Seal the nanopores Read more about anodizing
